seo.title: E-Commerce Data Extraction AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for data extraction AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for data extraction in e-commerce carries significant regulatory and financial risk — inaccurate product claims, pricing errors, or misrepresented return policies can trigger FTC enforcement, CCPA/GDPR penalties, and direct revenue loss. Schema-guided LLM extraction must be validated end-to-end to ensure every extracted field meets legal accuracy standards before it surfaces to customers or downstream systems. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls for teams building reliable, compliant data extraction pipelines in e-commerce environments.

sections.heading: Schema Design & Validation Controls
sections.description: Ensure your extraction schema is purpose-built for e-commerce data accuracy and enforces type, format, and completeness constraints before any data reaches production systems.

items.label: Define strict field-level data types for all product attributes
items.description: Enforce typed schemas (e.g., price as decimal with currency code, SKU as alphanumeric string, boolean for in-stock status) to prevent type coercion errors that cause incorrect pricing or availability display.

items.label: Require mandatory fields for legally sensitive attributes
items.description: Mark fields like price, return policy URL, product condition, and promotional terms as required in your schema so extraction jobs fail loudly rather than silently omit regulated data.

items.label: Implement enum constraints for categorical product fields
items.description: Use closed-value enumerations for fields like product condition (new/refurbished/used) and shipping class to prevent LLM hallucination of non-existent categories that could mislead customers.

items.label: Set numeric range validators on pricing fields
items.description: Apply min/max bounds and decimal precision rules to extracted price, discount, and tax fields to catch orders-of-magnitude errors before they propagate to checkout flows.

items.label: Version-control all extraction schemas with change audit logs
items.description: Maintain a versioned schema registry so any field definition change is traceable, reviewable, and does not silently alter downstream data contracts or compliance posture.

items.label: Validate extracted URLs for return/refund policy fields
items.description: Run regex and HTTP reachability checks on extracted policy URLs to ensure customers are never directed to broken or incorrect return policy pages, which constitutes a deceptive practice under FTC guidelines.

items.label: Test schema against adversarial document formats before deployment
items.description: Run extraction against malformed PDFs, HTML with nested tables, and multi-currency documents to confirm schema validation catches edge cases that LLMs may misparse.

sections.heading: Field Accuracy & Substantiation Verification
sections.description: Extracted product claims, pricing, and promotional data must be verifiable against source documents and substantiated before being published, per FTC advertising guidelines.

items.label: Implement source-grounding checks for all product claim fields
items.description: After extraction, verify that each product claim (e.g., '30% faster', 'clinically tested') maps to a specific span in the source document — flag any claim the LLM cannot ground to prevent unsubstantiated advertising.

items.label: Cross-validate extracted prices against authoritative price feeds
items.description: Compare LLM-extracted prices to your canonical pricing database or supplier feed on every extraction run; block publication of any price that deviates beyond a configurable threshold (e.g., ±1%).

items.label: Flag promotional terms for human review before activation
items.description: Route extracted promotional language (e.g., 'limited time', 'while supplies last', 'free shipping') to a human review queue before it goes live, since FTC rules require promotional claims to be accurate and non-deceptive.

items.label: Validate extracted discount percentages against base and sale prices
items.description: Programmatically recalculate discount percentages from extracted base and sale price fields and reject records where the stated discount does not match the computed value within a 0.5% tolerance.

items.label: Audit extracted return/refund policy text against merchant-provided policy documents
items.description: Diff extracted return policy summaries against the full policy document quarterly to ensure AI-summarized policies have not drifted from the legally binding terms customers are entitled to.

items.label: Log confidence scores for each extracted field and set rejection thresholds
items.description: Capture LLM confidence or logprob signals per field; automatically reject or quarantine records where confidence on price, condition, or policy fields falls below defined thresholds rather than publishing uncertain data.

items.label: Maintain an extraction audit trail linking output records to source documents
items.description: Store immutable links between each extracted record and its source document version so disputes about incorrect product data can be investigated and resolved with evidence.

sections.heading: Privacy, Data Handling & PCI DSS Compliance
sections.description: E-commerce data extraction pipelines frequently touch PII and payment-adjacent data; controls must prevent unauthorized processing, ensure consent alignment, and maintain PCI DSS scoping boundaries.

items.label: Exclude payment card data fields from all LLM extraction schemas
items.description: Explicitly block PANs, CVVs, and expiry dates from entering LLM context by applying pre-processing redaction before documents reach the extraction pipeline, maintaining PCI DSS scope minimization.

items.label: Classify extracted customer data fields by CCPA/GDPR sensitivity before storage
items.description: Tag any extracted field containing personal information (name, email, address, purchase history) with its regulatory classification at extraction time to enforce downstream retention and deletion obligations.

items.label: Implement data minimization — extract only fields required for the declared business purpose
items.description: Review your extraction schema against the stated use case quarterly and remove any fields that are not strictly necessary, reducing GDPR/CCPA exposure and LLM processing of unnecessary PII.

items.label: Ensure LLM API calls do not transmit PII to third-party model providers without DPA
items.description: Before routing documents containing customer PII to any external LLM API, confirm a Data Processing Agreement is in place and that the provider's data retention policies meet GDPR/CCPA requirements.

items.label: Apply role-based access controls to extracted data stores
items.description: Restrict access to raw extraction outputs containing PII or pricing data to only the systems and personnel with a documented need, with access logged for audit purposes.

items.label: Define and test data subject deletion workflows for extracted records
items.description: Verify that a CCPA/GDPR deletion request triggers removal of the subject's data from extraction outputs, intermediate stores, and any caches within the legally required timeframe.

items.label: Scan extraction pipeline logs for inadvertent PII capture before retention
items.description: Run automated PII detection (regex + NER) on pipeline logs before they are written to long-term storage to prevent accidental retention of customer data in operational logs.

sections.heading: Monitoring, Incident Response & Continuous Quality Assurance
sections.description: Ongoing monitoring of extraction quality and a defined incident response process are essential to catch pricing errors, policy drift, and model degradation before they cause customer harm or regulatory exposure.

items.label: Set up real-time alerts for extraction failure rates exceeding baseline thresholds
items.description: Configure monitoring to page on-call staff when schema validation failure rates exceed 2% of daily extraction volume, as spikes indicate document format changes or model drift that could propagate bad data.

items.label: Run a daily automated regression suite against a golden dataset of labeled e-commerce documents
items.description: Maintain a curated set of 200+ labeled source documents covering product types, pricing formats, and policy variations; run extraction against this set daily and alert on accuracy drops above 1%.

items.label: Define and document an incident response playbook for incorrect pricing publication
items.description: Create a step-by-step runbook covering detection, rollback, customer notification, and FTC/state AG reporting obligations for incidents where incorrect prices were displayed to customers.

items.label: Conduct monthly human-in-the-loop audits of a random sample of extracted records
items.description: Have a compliance-trained reviewer manually verify a statistically significant random sample (minimum 1% or 100 records, whichever is greater) of extraction outputs each month against source documents.

items.label: Track and trend field-level extraction accuracy metrics over time
items.description: Maintain dashboards showing per-field accuracy rates for price, product condition, return policy, and promotional terms so gradual model drift is visible before it causes compliance failures.

items.label: Implement a customer-facing error reporting mechanism for incorrect product data
items.description: Provide a clear, accessible channel for customers to report inaccurate product information; route reports to the extraction QA team within 24 hours and use them to update the golden dataset.

items.label: Schedule quarterly reviews of extraction prompts and schemas against updated regulations
items.description: Assign a compliance owner to review FTC guidance updates, CCPA/GDPR regulatory changes, and ADA accessibility requirements quarterly and update extraction schemas and prompts accordingly.

items.label: Document model version, prompt version, and schema version for every extraction batch
items.description: Log the exact LLM model version, system prompt hash, and schema version used for each extraction batch so any future compliance investigation can reproduce or explain the extraction behavior.

notes.note: Under 15 U.S.C. § 45, any product claim extracted and published by your AI agent must have a reasonable basis before publication. Extracted superlatives (e.g., 'best-in-class', 'clinically proven') must be grounded in the source document and reviewed by a human before going live; publishing unsubstantiated AI-extracted claims exposes the company to FTC enforcement action and civil penalties.

If your extraction pipeline processes California residents' or EU data subjects' personal information, you must disclose the use of automated processing in your privacy policy, provide opt-out rights for sale/sharing of extracted data, and honor deletion requests within 45 days (CCPA) or 30 days (GDPR). Extraction pipelines that ingest customer-generated documents (e.g., order confirmations, reviews) are in scope.

notes.regulation: PCI DSS v4.0 — Scope Minimization for Payment Data
notes.note: PCI DSS Requirement 3.2 prohibits storage of sensitive authentication data post-authorization. Any document ingested by your extraction pipeline that may contain payment card numbers must be pre-screened and redacted before LLM processing. Routing cardholder data through an LLM API expands your PCI DSS scope to include that provider and requires a formal scope assessment and potentially a QSA review.

notes.regulation: ADA / WCAG 2.1 — Accessibility of Extracted Content
notes.note: Product data extracted and rendered on your e-commerce storefront must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards under ADA Title III obligations. Ensure extracted product descriptions include alt-text-ready content, that price and availability data is structured for screen reader compatibility, and that AI-generated product summaries do not introduce jargon or formatting that degrades accessibility for users with disabilities.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously monitor your e-commerce data extraction agents against FTC, CCPA, and PCI DSS requirements — catching pricing errors, policy drift, and schema violations before they reach your customers.
